parameters.name: x-api-version
parameters.description: API version. Required; pinned to this document.

address.description: Token contract address (format depends on the chain)

schema.description: Supported chains and tokens, keyed by numeric chain id

traceId.description: Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.

get.description: Get supported chains and tokens with additional metadata

parameters.name: x-api-version
parameters.description: API version. Required; pinned to this document.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: API key.

parameters.name: sourceChainId
parameters.description: Source chain ID

parameters.name: sourceToken
parameters.description: Source token address

parameters.name: destinationChainId
parameters.description: Destination chain ID

parameters.name: destinationToken

schema.description: Destination token address (EVM 0x or non-EVM base58)

parameters.description: Destination token address (EVM 0x or non-EVM base58)

unlimited.description: True when an uncapped settlement layer (e.g. OFT) supports this route

maxAmount.description: Largest known fillable amount for this token by a single relayer instance. Exact for relayer-market settlement layers (ACROSS, ECO); bridge-backed layers (Relay, NEAR, Rhino) return a conservative probed lower bound. Null when unlimited.

traceId.description: Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.
Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.
Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.

get.description: Returns the largest known fillable amount for a single deposit on the given source→destination route. Relayer-market layers return exact capacity; bridge-backed layers return a conservative probed lower bound.

parameters.name: x-api-version
parameters.description: API version. Required; pinned to this document.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: API key.

parameters.name: id

schema.description: Unique identifier of the intent operation

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the intent operation

parameters.name: full

schema.description: Whether to include intent operation details

parameters.description: Whether to include intent operation details

status.description: Overall intent status: PENDING, COMPLETED, or FAILED

createdAt.description: Intent creation time in unix seconds

latencyMs.description: Time from creation to the latest leg landing, in ms. Null until any leg lands.

settlementLayer.description: Settlement layer; null if the routing strategy is unrecognized

items.description: A token moved on this leg

tokens.description: All tokens moved on this leg

txHash.description: Transaction hash (present once the leg lands)

timestamp.description: Block timestamp in unix seconds (present once landed)

items.description: A single source or destination leg
A token moved on this leg

tokens.description: All tokens moved on this leg

txHash.description: Transaction hash (present once the leg lands)

timestamp.description: Block timestamp in unix seconds (present once landed)

destination.description: Destination (fill) leg; null before a fill is dispatched

chain.description: Chain ID the call executes on

phase.description: PRE_CLAIM runs on the origin chain before funds are claimed; DESTINATION runs on the destination chain after funds are delivered.

value.description: Native value sent with the call, in wei (base units)

items.description: A single call executed by the intent

executions.description: Calls the intent executes — preClaim ops on their origin chain and destination ops on the destination chain. The signed intended calls; no per-call result is recorded. Empty for token-only intents with no custom calls.

sponsored.description: Whether gas/fees were sponsored for this intent

sponsoredValue.description: Sponsored value in fee-token base units

protocolFee.description: Protocol fee in fee-token base units

cost.description: Cost summary from the recorded fee sponsorship. Amounts are raw base units; omitted when no sponsorship row exists.

details.description: Extended intent details, returned only when `full=true`

claimTimestamp.description: Block timestamp of the claim in unix seconds

destinationChainId.description: Destination (fill) chain ID

fillTimestamp.description: Block timestamp of the fill in unix seconds

traceId.description: Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.

get.description: Retrieves the status of an intent along with per-claim progress across chains.

parameters.name: x-api-version
parameters.description: API version. Required; pinned to this document.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: API key.

balance.description: Top-level aggregate balance. `unlocked` is the raw sum of per-chain unlocked balances — numerically meaningful only when per-chain decimals match.

tokenDecimals.description: Token decimals, taken from the first per-chain entry

chainId.description: Chain ID where the token balance exists

balance.description: Locked and unlocked balance on this chain

tokenAddress.description: Token contract address on this chain

traceId.description: Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.
Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.
Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.

get.description: Retrieves the token balances across all supported chains for a user's abstracted account

parameters.name: x-api-version
parameters.description: API version. Required; pinned to this document.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: API key.

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response (`pagination.nextCursor`). Omit for the first page.

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response (`pagination.nextCursor`). Omit for the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max items per page (max 100)

fromChains.description: Source chain IDs — one per distinct chain the intent spends on

token.description: Intent value token. The token delivered on the destination chain, or the spent token for same-chain intents (which have no persisted delivery).

amount.description: Intent value in base units — delivered on the destination chain, or spent for same-chain intents (which have no persisted delivery; for same-chain swaps this is the input, not the received amount).

createdAt.description: Intent creation time in unix seconds

data.description: Intents on this page, newest first

nextCursor.description: Pass as `cursor` to fetch the next page; null when no more results.

schema.description: Paginated list of the client’s intents

traceId.description: Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.
Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.
Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of the authenticated client's intents, newest first.

parameters.name: x-api-version
parameters.description: API version. Required; pinned to this document.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: API key.

id.description: Nonce of the intent operations, used as identifier

traceId.description: Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.
Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.

404.description: The quoted intent has expired or was already submitted. Request a new route to retry.

traceId.description: Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.
Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.

post.description: Submits a quoted intent for execution. Takes the `intentId` from `POST /quotes` (`routes[].intentId`) plus signatures (origin, destination, optionally target-execution) and optional EIP-7702 authorizations.

sponsor.description: Account whose funds the intent operation will spend. Identical to the account that signed the origin signatures — this field is named `sponsor` because in TheCompact it sponsors the resource-lock claim, not because there's a separate gas-sponsor party.

nonce.description: Intent operation nonce — a one-time identifier scoped to the orchestrator's intent registry, not an EVM transaction nonce. Used to prevent replay of the same quote.

targetExecutionNonce.description: Nonce for the optional target-execution signature (smart-session flows). Omitted for EOA accounts.

expires.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) after which the orchestrator will refuse to submit this intent.

arbiter.description: Contract that adjudicates settlement on this chain (TheCompact arbiter).

chainId.description: Chain ID where the element is executed

idsAndAmounts.description: Resource-locked balances pledged on this chain, as `[ERC-6909 tokenId, amount]` pairs.

spendTokens.description: Subset of `idsAndAmounts` actually drawn down to fund this element. Same `[ERC-6909 tokenId, amount]` shape.

recipient.description: Address that receives the output tokens on the destination chain.

tokenOut.description: Tokens to deliver on the destination chain, as `[ERC-6909 tokenId, amount]` pairs.

destinationChainId.description: Target chain ID for the mandate

fillDeadline.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) after which the destination fill is no longer valid.

vt.description: ERC-7579 encoded execution mode (first byte) and signature mode (second byte) for `destinationOps` / `preClaimOps`. Pre-computed by the orchestrator — clients pass through unchanged.

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

preClaimOps.description: Origin-chain ops that run immediately before funds are claimed by the relayer (e.g. user approvals, vault withdraws).

vt.description: ERC-7579 encoded execution mode (first byte) and signature mode (second byte) for `destinationOps` / `preClaimOps`. Pre-computed by the orchestrator — clients pass through unchanged.

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

destinationOps.description: Destination-chain ops that run after funds are delivered (e.g. user's target contract call).

using7579.description: true for ERC7579 smart accounts, false for EOAs (MULTICALL execution)

overhead.description: Overhead gas amount add to gas spent when executing destination ops

exchangeRate.description: Exchange rate between token selected for gas refund and native token consumed for gas

token.description: token address which will be used for gas refunding

prefundAmount.description: Native token amount the relayer sends to the user before execution, e.g. the LayerZero `lzFee` required by OFT bridges. In wei.

token.description: ERC20 token the relayer sends to the user before execution

amount.description: ERC20 token amount in smallest units

erc20Prefund.description: ERC20 token amounts the relayer sends to the user before execution, e.g. quote-embedded bridge fees sponsored via FeeCollector.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

requestId.description: Relay.link request ID. Use against Relay.link's status API to track the destination-chain fill.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

depositAddress.description: NEAR Intents deposit address. Track fill status via the NEAR Intents status API keyed on this address.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

commitmentId.description: Rhino.fi commitment ID. Use against Rhino.fi's status API to track the destination-chain fill.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

sourceDomainId.description: Circle CCTP source domain ID — needed to fetch the attestation for the burn message on the source chain.

bridgeFill.description: Bridge-as-fill metadata for cross-chain delivery tracking

bridgeDestinationOps.description: Marks this IE element as the post-bridge destination-ops executor for a bridge-with-destination-ops intent. Distinguishes from same-chain IE elements (both have `bridgeFill === undefined`).

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

requestId.description: Relay.link request ID. Use against Relay.link's status API to track the destination-chain fill.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

depositAddress.description: NEAR Intents deposit address. Track fill status via the NEAR Intents status API keyed on this address.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

commitmentId.description: Rhino.fi commitment ID. Use against Rhino.fi's status API to track the destination-chain fill.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

sourceDomainId.description: Circle CCTP source domain ID — needed to fetch the attestation for the burn message on the source chain.

bridgeFill.description: Bridge-as-fill metadata for cross-chain delivery tracking

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

provingFee.description: Hyperlane relay fee for proof dispatch (in destination chain native token)

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

requestId.description: Relay.link request ID for this route. Surfaces in the relayer-market broadcast — opaque to the client.

orderId.description: Relay v2 order ID used in the deposit contract calldata

subsidizedAmount.description: Relay settlement layer subsidized amount on the intent (denomination in USDC)

multicallData.description: ABI-encoded params for IRelayRouterV3.multicall, used for origin swaps via Relay contracts

executionValidationGas.description: Gas overhead the relayer must reserve for validating the execution on the origin chain. Used internally for origin gas accounting.

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

depositAddress.description: NEAR-provided deposit address on the origin chain

correlationId.description: NEAR correlation ID for tracking the intent

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

quoteId.description: Rhino.fi quote identifier — committed quote that becomes the commitmentId for the on-chain deposit

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

encodedVal.description: ABI-encoded settlement-layer witness data. Format depends on `settlementContext.settlementLayer`. Clients should treat this as opaque bytes and pass through unchanged.

qualifier.description: Settlement-layer-specific witness data binding this mandate to a relayer route.

minGas.description: Minimum gas reserved for pre-claim ops on this chain, in gas units. Packed with `preClaimGasStipend` as `uint128` for the settlement contract.

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

swapDestinations.description: Swap execution data for arbitrary output tokens, aligned by index with tokenOut

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

swapOrigins.description: Swap execution data for arbitrary input tokens, keyed by spendToken address

bundleId.description: Per-element nonce override used when this element is dispatched as its own intent (post-bridge destination ops). Unset elements fall back to the bundle-level nonce. Today only the destination-ops element on a bridge + destination-ops bundle sets this.

items.description: A single chain-leg of the intent. Each element pins the spend tokens on one origin chain to a destination mandate.

elements.description: Per-chain legs of the bundle. One element per origin chain involved in fulfilling the destination mandate.

serverSignature.description: HMAC digest of the bundle's canonical encoding. Set by the orchestrator at quote time and verified at submit to ensure the bundle wasn't tampered with between calls.

strategy.description: Planning strategy that produced this bundle (e.g. `ACROSS`, `RELAY`, `INTENT_EXECUTOR`). Pinned in metadata so the submission path can dispatch to the matching execution code.

schemeId.description: Serialization scheme version for this bundle (e.g. `v1`). Used to migrate the bundle format without breaking in-flight quotes.

tokenPrices.description: Snapshot of token-to-USD prices at quote time. Pinned here so cost figures remain consistent across the quote-submit window.

gasPrices.description: Gas prices per chain in wei

opGasParams.description: OP-stack L1-fee parameters (`l1BaseFee`, `l1BlobBaseFee`, scalars) per chain at quote time. Used to reproduce the L1 data-cost component of OP-stack chain gas — pinned so the cost does not drift between quote and submit.

accountType.description: Account type. Blanc clients should use `ERC7579`; the legacy `smartAccount` value is equivalent and accepted for alps wire compatibility, and gets rewritten to `ERC7579` by the `alpsAccountType` versioning change before Zod validation.

setupOps.description: Setup operations for the smart account. Only used if the account is not deployed

isDeployed.description: Whether the account is deployed on this chain

isERC7579.description: Whether the account supports ERC7579 standard

contract.description: address of contract to which delegation on behalf of sponsor will be assumed for given chain ID

requiredDelegations.description: Map of chain -> EOA and delegated address required for user to sign for 7702 authorizations

address.description: Recipient address. Format depends on destination chain — 0x-hex for EVM destinations, base58 for Solana, T-address for Tron.

accountType.description: Account type. Blanc clients should use `ERC7579`; the legacy `smartAccount` value is equivalent and accepted for alps wire compatibility, and gets rewritten to `ERC7579` by the `alpsAccountType` versioning change before Zod validation.

setupOps.description: Setup operations for the smart account. Only used if the account is not deployed

isDeployed.description: Whether the account is deployed on this chain

isERC7579.description: Whether the account supports ERC7579 standard

contract.description: address of contract to which delegation on behalf of sponsor will be assumed for given chain ID

requiredDelegations.description: Map of chain -> EOA and delegated address required for user to sign for 7702 authorizations

protocolFees.description: Protocol fees by token address (in token units)

feeBps.description: Integrator app-fee rate in basis points.

baseAmount.description: Principal amount the app fee was computed from, in token base units.

amount.description: App-fee amount in token base units.

chainId.description: Origin chain ID where the app-fee token is spent.

tokenAddress.description: Address of the token used to pay the app fee.

appFee.description: Concrete user-paid integrator app-fee leg. App fees are never sponsored.

gas.description: Whether to sponsor gas for the intent

bridgeFees.description: Whether to sponsor bridge fees for the intent

swapFees.description: Whether to sponsor swap fees for the intent

sponsorSettings.description: Sponsor settings requested at quote time. Used during submit to distinguish sponsored zero-segment setup-only fills from unsponsored fallback fills.

executionTokensReceived.description: Tokens that will be received by EOA executions. These will be swept to the recipient account.

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

preClaimExecutions.description: User-supplied pre-claim executions included in the bundle, keyed by chain ID.

accounting.description: Accounting facts derived from the selected plan and used for IntentOperation persistence. Excluded from the legacy serverSignature HMAC surface for rolling-deploy compatibility; submit integrity comes from the stored quote session.

signedMetadata.description: Signed metadata containing prices, fees, and gas parameters

chainId.description: Chain ID for EIP-7702 delegation, 0 means it can be applied to any chain

address.description: Address of the delegate for EIP-7702 delegation

signedAuthorizations.description: List of 7702 authorizations signed by EOA matching sponsor

chainId.description: Chain ID for EIP-7702 delegation, 0 means it can be applied to any chain

address.description: Address of the delegate for EIP-7702 delegation

recipientSignedAuthorizations.description: List of 7702 authorizations signed by EOA matching recipient account (if present)

parameters.name: x-api-version
parameters.description: API version. Required; pinned to this document.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: API key.

intents.description: Array of intents, each mapping token addresses to amounts that can be filled by a single relayer

traceId.description: Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.
Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.

422.description: Insufficient liquidity to fill the full amount. Partial splits returned.

unfillable.description: Token amounts that cannot be filled

traceId.description: Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.

post.description: Splits token amounts into multiple intents based on available relayer liquidity. Each returned intent can be filled by a single relayer.

chainId.description: The chain ID the intents settle on

tokens.description: Map of token addresses to amounts

settlementLayers.description: Which settlement layers the orchestrator may use. `{ include: [...] }` (allow-list) or `{ exclude: [...] }` (deny-list, inverted against the orchestrator's live layer set); a bare array means `include`. Internal modes (`SAME_CHAIN`, `INTENT_EXECUTOR`) are not selectable. Default unset = all layers eligible.

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

depositAddress.description: NEAR Intents deposit address. Track fill status via the NEAR Intents status API keyed on this address.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

commitmentId.description: Rhino.fi commitment ID. Use against Rhino.fi's status API to track the destination-chain fill.

destinationChainId.description: Destination chain ID for the bridge fill

fillExpirationPeriod.description: Optional bridge-specific fill deadline duration, in seconds. Preserved on stored quote reload so checksum validation uses the same bridgeFill payload that was signed at quote time.

sourceDomainId.description: Circle CCTP source domain ID — needed to fetch the attestation for the burn message on the source chain.

bridgeFill.description: Bridge-as-fill metadata for cross-chain delivery tracking

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

provingFee.description: Hyperlane relay fee for proof dispatch (in destination chain native token)

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

requestId.description: Relay.link request ID for this route. Surfaces in the relayer-market broadcast — opaque to the client.

orderId.description: Relay v2 order ID used in the deposit contract calldata

subsidizedAmount.description: Relay settlement layer subsidized amount on the intent (denomination in USDC)

multicallData.description: ABI-encoded params for IRelayRouterV3.multicall, used for origin swaps via Relay contracts

executionValidationGas.description: Gas overhead the relayer must reserve for validating the execution on the origin chain. Used internally for origin gas accounting.

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

depositAddress.description: NEAR-provided deposit address on the origin chain

correlationId.description: NEAR correlation ID for tracking the intent

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

quoteId.description: Rhino.fi quote identifier — committed quote that becomes the commitmentId for the on-chain deposit

using7579.description: True when the originating account is ERC-7579-compatible (smart account). False for EOAs — those settle via MULTICALL execution.

fundingMethod.description: How the relayer pulls the user's funds on the origin chain. `PERMIT2` for EIP-712 Permit2 approvals; `NO_FUNDING` when the account itself executes the transfer (INTENT_EXECUTOR path).

encodedVal.description: ABI-encoded settlement-layer witness data. Format depends on `settlementContext.settlementLayer`. Clients should treat this as opaque bytes and pass through unchanged.

qualifier.description: Settlement-layer-specific witness data binding this mandate to a relayer route.

minGas.description: Minimum gas reserved for pre-claim ops on this chain, in gas units. Packed with `preClaimGasStipend` as `uint128` for the settlement contract.

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

swapDestinations.description: Swap execution data for arbitrary output tokens, aligned by index with tokenOut

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

swapOrigins.description: Swap execution data for arbitrary input tokens, keyed by spendToken address

bundleId.description: Per-element nonce override used when this element is dispatched as its own intent (post-bridge destination ops). Unset elements fall back to the bundle-level nonce. Today only the destination-ops element on a bridge + destination-ops bundle sets this.

items.description: A single chain-leg of the intent. Each element pins the spend tokens on one origin chain to a destination mandate.

elements.description: Per-chain legs of the bundle. One element per origin chain involved in fulfilling the destination mandate.

serverSignature.description: HMAC digest of the bundle's canonical encoding. Set by the orchestrator at quote time and verified at submit to ensure the bundle wasn't tampered with between calls.

strategy.description: Planning strategy that produced this bundle (e.g. `ACROSS`, `RELAY`, `INTENT_EXECUTOR`). Pinned in metadata so the submission path can dispatch to the matching execution code.

schemeId.description: Serialization scheme version for this bundle (e.g. `v1`). Used to migrate the bundle format without breaking in-flight quotes.

tokenPrices.description: Snapshot of token-to-USD prices at quote time. Pinned here so cost figures remain consistent across the quote-submit window.

gasPrices.description: Gas prices per chain in wei

opGasParams.description: OP-stack L1-fee parameters (`l1BaseFee`, `l1BlobBaseFee`, scalars) per chain at quote time. Used to reproduce the L1 data-cost component of OP-stack chain gas — pinned so the cost does not drift between quote and submit.

accountType.description: Account type. Blanc clients should use `ERC7579`; the legacy `smartAccount` value is equivalent and accepted for alps wire compatibility, and gets rewritten to `ERC7579` by the `alpsAccountType` versioning change before Zod validation.

setupOps.description: Setup operations for the smart account. Only used if the account is not deployed

isDeployed.description: Whether the account is deployed on this chain

isERC7579.description: Whether the account supports ERC7579 standard

contract.description: address of contract to which delegation on behalf of sponsor will be assumed for given chain ID

requiredDelegations.description: Map of chain -> EOA and delegated address required for user to sign for 7702 authorizations

address.description: Recipient address. Format depends on destination chain — 0x-hex for EVM destinations, base58 for Solana, T-address for Tron.

accountType.description: Account type. Blanc clients should use `ERC7579`; the legacy `smartAccount` value is equivalent and accepted for alps wire compatibility, and gets rewritten to `ERC7579` by the `alpsAccountType` versioning change before Zod validation.

setupOps.description: Setup operations for the smart account. Only used if the account is not deployed

isDeployed.description: Whether the account is deployed on this chain

isERC7579.description: Whether the account supports ERC7579 standard

contract.description: address of contract to which delegation on behalf of sponsor will be assumed for given chain ID

requiredDelegations.description: Map of chain -> EOA and delegated address required for user to sign for 7702 authorizations

protocolFees.description: Protocol fees by token address (in token units)

feeBps.description: Integrator app-fee rate in basis points.

baseAmount.description: Principal amount the app fee was computed from, in token base units.

amount.description: App-fee amount in token base units.

chainId.description: Origin chain ID where the app-fee token is spent.

tokenAddress.description: Address of the token used to pay the app fee.

appFee.description: Concrete user-paid integrator app-fee leg. App fees are never sponsored.

sponsorSettings.description: Sponsor settings echoed back on the older wire

executionTokensReceived.description: Tokens that will be received by EOA executions. These will be swept to the recipient account.

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

preClaimExecutions.description: User-supplied pre-claim executions included in the bundle, keyed by chain ID.

accounting.description: Accounting facts derived from the selected plan and used for IntentOperation persistence. Excluded from the legacy serverSignature HMAC surface for rolling-deploy compatibility; submit integrity comes from the stored quote session.

chainId.description: Chain ID for EIP-7702 delegation, 0 means it can be applied to any chain

address.description: Address of the delegate for EIP-7702 delegation

signedAuthorizations.description: List of 7702 authorizations signed by EOA matching sponsor

chainId.description: Chain ID for EIP-7702 delegation, 0 means it can be applied to any chain

address.description: Address of the delegate for EIP-7702 delegation

recipientSignedAuthorizations.description: List of 7702 authorizations signed by EOA matching recipient account (if present)

intentOp.description: Signed bundle ready for submission. Carries the per-chain legs, server signature, and quote metadata.

hasFulfilledAll.description: All requested output tokens can be fully delivered by this route.

locked.description: Amount drawn from the user's resource-locked balance, in the token's smallest unit.

unlocked.description: Amount drawn from the user's regular (unlocked) ERC-20 balance, in the token's smallest unit.

tokensSpent.description: Per-chain, per-token amounts the user is spending. `locked` = drawn from the resource-locked balance (TheCompact); `unlocked` = drawn from regular ERC-20 balance via approval. Sum is the total user debit on that token.

tokenAddress.description: Contract address of the delivered token (EVM 0x or non-EVM base58).

amountSpent.description: Total amount of this token spent at origin to deliver `destinationAmount` at destination. Includes fees.

destinationAmount.description: Amount the user requested. Delivered amount = `destinationAmount` (when `hasFulfilled` is true).

fee.description: Total fee charged in this token's smallest unit — sum of `feeBreakdown`.

gasFee.description: Combined gas fees: destination fill, destination swap execution, and origin chain gas

bridgeFee.description: Cross-chain bridge commission charged by the relayer

swapFee.description: Solver swap commission for cross-token conversions

appFee.description: Integrator app fee charged in the fee-bearing input token

hyperCoreActivationFee.description: Hyperliquid HyperCore activation fee — 1 USDC charged on the first inbound deposit to a non-activated HyperCore account. Equal to 1_000_000 (USDC 6-decimal wei) when the orchestrator detects an unactivated HyperCore recipient and inflates the user's USDC delivery to cover the fee; 0 otherwise. Always present, like every other field in the breakdown.

feeBreakdown.description: Per-category breakdown of fees in token units (BPS-derived computed values)

gasFee.description: Combined gas fees: destination fill, destination swap execution, and origin chain gas

bridgeFee.description: Cross-chain bridge commission charged by the relayer

swapFee.description: Solver swap commission for cross-token conversions

appFee.description: Integrator app fee charged in the fee-bearing input token

hyperCoreActivationFee.description: Hyperliquid HyperCore activation fee — 1 USDC charged on the first inbound deposit to a non-activated HyperCore account. Equal to 1_000_000 (USDC 6-decimal wei) when the orchestrator detects an unactivated HyperCore recipient and inflates the user's USDC delivery to cover the fee; 0 otherwise. Always present, like every other field in the breakdown.

breakdown.description: Per-category breakdown of fees in token units (BPS-derived computed values)

additionalProperties.description: Fee entry for a specific token with per-category breakdown

feesByToken.description: Fees paid in tokens other than the output token (e.g., settlement fees, origin gas in input token)

hasFulfilled.description: True when the route fully delivers `destinationAmount` for this token.

tokensReceived.description: Per-token delivery summary on the destination chain.

feeBps.description: Integrator app-fee rate in basis points.

baseAmount.description: Principal amount the app fee was computed from, in token base units.

amount.description: App-fee amount in token base units.

chainId.description: Origin chain ID where the app-fee token is spent.

tokenAddress.description: Address of the token used to pay the app fee.

appFee.description: The single whole-intent integrator app-fee carve (token/chain/amount), or `null` when no app fee is charged. App fees are never sponsored.

chainId.description: Chain ID where the gas cost was incurred
Chain ID where the gas cost was incurred

destination.description: Gas cost on the destination chain

totalUSD.description: Total gas cost in USD across all chains

gasCost.description: Breakdown of gas costs across origin and destination chains

gasFeeUSD.description: Total gas fees in USD (destination fill, swap execution, origin gas)

swapFeeUSD.description: Total solver swap commission in USD

settlementFeeUSD.description: Total settlement layer fee in USD

appFeeUSD.description: Total integrator app fee in USD

totalFeeUSD.description: Sum of all fees in USD (including sponsored)

feeBreakdownUSD.description: Aggregate fee breakdown in USD across all tokens and chains

hasFulfilledAll.description: One or more requested output tokens fall short on this route. See `tokenShortfall` for details.

tokenAddress.description: Address of the token that has not been fulfilled (EVM 0x or non-EVM base58)

destinationAmount.description: Target amount of the token that has not been fulfilled

amountSpent.description: Amount spent on the token that has not been fulfilled

fee.description: Fee associated with the token that has not been fulfilled

gasFee.description: Combined gas fees: destination fill, destination swap execution, and origin chain gas

bridgeFee.description: Cross-chain bridge commission charged by the relayer

swapFee.description: Solver swap commission for cross-token conversions

appFee.description: Integrator app fee charged in the fee-bearing input token

hyperCoreActivationFee.description: Hyperliquid HyperCore activation fee — 1 USDC charged on the first inbound deposit to a non-activated HyperCore account. Equal to 1_000_000 (USDC 6-decimal wei) when the orchestrator detects an unactivated HyperCore recipient and inflates the user's USDC delivery to cover the fee; 0 otherwise. Always present, like every other field in the breakdown.

breakdown.description: Per-category breakdown of fees in token units (BPS-derived computed values)

additionalProperties.description: Fee entry for a specific token with per-category breakdown

feesByToken.description: Fees paid in tokens other than the output token (e.g., settlement fees, origin gas in input token)

tokenSymbol.description: Symbol of the token that has not been fulfilled

decimals.description: Number of decimal places for the token

tokenShortfall.description: List of tokens that have not been fulfilled

totalTokenShortfallInUSD.description: Total shortfall of tokens in USD

intentCost.description: Cost breakdown for the route. Discriminated on `hasFulfilledAll`: when `true`, the requested amounts can be delivered; when `false`, the route falls short and `tokenShortfall` lists the missing tokens.

type.description: Discriminator: `approval` means the user must ERC-20 approve `spender` for at least `amount` before submission.

amount.description: Minimum required allowance, in the token's smallest unit.

spender.description: Always the canonical Permit2 contract on this chain. The EOA approves Permit2; the settlement contract pulls funds via Permit2 at claim time.

type.description: Discriminator: `wrap` means the user must wrap native ETH into the chain's WETH-equivalent before submission.

amount.description: Minimum amount to wrap, in wei.

tokenRequirements.description: Pre-flight token operations the user must perform before submitting this route (approvals, wrapping). Emitted for EOA accounts only.

schema.description: Response body for `POST /quotes` on the older API version

traceId.description: Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.
Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.
Trace ID for request correlation. Returned in x-trace-id for all responses and in legacy alps error bodies for compatibility.

post.description: Computes a quote for an intent: token transfers, target executions, and cost breakdown. Returns a pre-ranked `routes[]` with per-route `intentId` to pass to `POST /intents` for execution.

destinationChainId.description: The ID of the target blockchain

tokenAddress.description: The address of the requested token. Format depends on destination chain — 0x-hex for EVM, base58 SPL mint for Solana, T-address for Tron.

amount.description: The amount of the requested token (in the smallest unit). Omit for max-out.

balance.description: HyperCore balance class for this token request — 'spot' or 'perp'. Optional; defaults to 'perp' when destinationChainId is HyperCore. Rejected on every other destination.

tokenRequests.description: A list of token requested on the target chain

accountType.description: Account type. Blanc clients should use `ERC7579`; the legacy `smartAccount` value is equivalent and accepted for alps wire compatibility, and gets rewritten to `ERC7579` by the `alpsAccountType` versioning change before Zod validation.

setupOps.description: Setup operations for the smart account. Only used if the account is not deployed

mockSignatures.description: Per-chain stub signatures used only during gas estimation. Required for smart-session flows where the real signature length depends on session policy — the orchestrator needs a same-shape placeholder to simulate the verification cost. Keys are chain IDs as decimal strings; `"0"` is a cross-chain fallback applied when no chain-specific entry matches.

mockSignature.description: Deprecated. Must be omitted; use `mockSignatures` (keyed by chain id) instead.

contract.description: address of contract to which delegation on behalf of sponsor will be assumed for given chain ID

delegations.description: Per-chain specific map to delegated contract address for 7702 delegations. Use `0` to indicate cross-chain delegation

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

destinationExecutions.description: Execution calls on the target chain.

value.description: Amount of ETH (in wei) sent in the execution

preClaimExecutions.description: Execution calls to perform before the claim on each origin chain, keyed by chain ID. Max 10 ops per chain, max 5 chains.

destinationGasLimit.description: The gas limit for the target chain executions

accountAccessList.description: Account access list specifying which chains and tokens an account may access

address.description: Recipient address. Format depends on destination chain — 0x-hex for EVM destinations, base58 for Solana, T-address for Tron.

accountType.description: Account type. Blanc clients should use `ERC7579`; the legacy `smartAccount` value is equivalent and accepted for alps wire compatibility, and gets rewritten to `ERC7579` by the `alpsAccountType` versioning change before Zod validation.

setupOps.description: Setup operations for the smart account. Only used if the account is not deployed

mockSignatures.description: Per-chain stub signatures used only during gas estimation. Required for smart-session flows where the real signature length depends on session policy — the orchestrator needs a same-shape placeholder to simulate the verification cost. Keys are chain IDs as decimal strings; `"0"` is a cross-chain fallback applied when no chain-specific entry matches.

mockSignature.description: Deprecated. Must be omitted; use `mockSignatures` (keyed by chain id) instead.

contract.description: address of contract to which delegation on behalf of sponsor will be assumed for given chain ID

delegations.description: Per-chain specific map to delegated contract address for 7702 delegations. Use `0` to indicate cross-chain delegation

settlementLayers.description: Which settlement layers the orchestrator may use. `{ include: [...] }` (allow-list) or `{ exclude: [...] }` (deny-list, inverted against the orchestrator's live layer set); a bare array means `include`. Internal modes (`SAME_CHAIN`, `INTENT_EXECUTOR`) are not selectable. Default unset = all layers eligible.

gas.description: Whether to sponsor gas for the intent

bridgeFees.description: Whether to sponsor bridge fees for the intent

swapFees.description: Whether to sponsor swap fees for the intent

signatureMode.description: How the user's intent signature will be verified onchain. `ECDSA` for plain EOA signatures; `ERC1271_EMISSARY` for smart-account signatures verified via TheCompact emissary delegation. The orchestrator picks a default based on `account.accountType` — only set this if you need to override.

feeToken.description: Reserved for future use. No effect today.

feeBps.description: App fee rate in basis points of the input value (0–10000 = 0–100%).

executionTokensReceived.description: Tokens that will be received by EOA executions. These will be swept to the recipient account.

auxiliaryFunds.description: Additional balances the quote should treat as available, beyond what is currently on the account. Use this to get a preliminary quote against funds you can produce by the time you submit — e.g. liquidity in a DeFi vault you will withdraw, an in-flight CEX deposit, or a parallel transfer from another wallet. Keyed by chain ID, then token address; amounts in the token's smallest unit.

selectionStrategy.description: How to rank candidate plans. `cheapest` minimizes direct USD cost. `fastest` minimizes estimated fill time (with cost tiebreaker). `best` balances both via a notional- and time-weighted shadow fee.
